steps.title: Read the longing
steps.content: The poem centers on the speaker's desire for the ocean to bring rest: the speaker asks the waves to rock them to sleep, carry them away from toil and care, and turn wave sounds into a soothing lullaby.

steps.title: Reject unsupported purposes
steps.content: Choice B makes daily life a full contrast, but the poem only briefly mentions toil and care. Choice C invents increasing intensity and overstates the brief reference to hardship. Choice D claims the speaker has qualms, though no hesitation appears. The remaining choice captures the longing for the ocean to create inner calm. The answer is A.
